rationale: A confirmed US-Iran ceasefire with Strait of Hormuz reopening is a qualitative new development that could ease energy prices for Poland — a meaningful indirect positive impact.

top_events.title: US, Iran announce ceasefire agreement; Strait of Hormuz reopening
top_events.summary: A confirmed US-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening could lower global oil and gas prices, easing energy costs for Polish households and industry. This is a qualitative new development vs. prior background of unresolved tensions.

top_events.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
top_events.summary: Anti-G7 protests in Geneva are distant noise for Polish daily life; no direct policy or security impact on Poland.

top_events.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
top_events.summary: A large UK-Japan bilateral investment deal has negligible direct impact on Polish residents; minor background signal for EU trade environment.
